original_abstract:
Traffic forecasting serves as a cornerstone of multivariate time series analysis and remains a focal point of research within the AI community. To effectively capture the complex spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity inherent in traffic streams, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning, a novel graph structure learning framework designed specifically for spatio-temporal data. We instantiate this approach through the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN), which integrates a Meta-Graph Learner—supported by a specialized Meta-Node Bank—directly into a GCRN encoder-decoder architecture. 

Our framework underwent rigorous evaluation across two standard benchmarks, METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, as well as a newly introduced large-scale dataset, EXPY-TKY, which encompasses 1,843 expressway links in Tokyo. Experimental results demonstrate that MegaCRN consistently outperforms current state-of-the-art models across all three datasets. Furthermore, qualitative analysis reveals that our model successfully disentangles distinct patterns among road links and time intervals, exhibiting robust adaptability to anomalous traffic conditions. The code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
humanized_abstract:
Traffic forecasting is one of the core tasks in multivariate time series analysis and is still a hot research area in the AI community. Forecasting traffic flows efficiently and accurately for capturing the underlying spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity remains a challenging task. In this work, we propose a new graph structure learning framework named Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning for spatio-temporal data, and propose a new network named Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN) to verify the superiority of the proposed framework. MegaCRN consists of a GCRN encoder-decoder structure and the Meta-Graph Learner with a Meta-Node Bank.

The proposed framework is extensively evaluated on widely used METR-LA and PEMS-BAY as well as a new large-scale EXPY-TKY dataset. Experiment results show that MegaCRN not only surpasses all the existing state-of-the-art models on three different benchmarks, but also brings some interesting insights. It is demonstrated that our proposed model can decompose varied underlying distributions of different road links and time slots while meanwhile showing robust adaptability to a wide range of anomalous scenarios. Code and datasets are available in the following repository: https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.

document.output:
Traffic forecasting is one of the core tasks in multivariate time series analysis and is still a hot research area in the AI community. Forecasting traffic flows efficiently and accurately for capturing the underlying spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity remains a challenging task. In this work, we propose a new graph structure learning framework named Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning for spatio-temporal data, and propose a new network named Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN) to verify the superiority of the proposed framework. MegaCRN consists of a GCRN encoder-decoder structure and the Meta-Graph Learner with a Meta-Node Bank.

The proposed framework is extensively evaluated on widely used METR-LA and PEMS-BAY as well as a new large-scale EXPY-TKY dataset. Experiment results show that MegaCRN not only surpasses all the existing state-of-the-art models on three different benchmarks, but also brings some interesting insights. It is demonstrated that our proposed model can decompose varied underlying distributions of different road links and time slots while meanwhile showing robust adaptability to a wide range of anomalous scenarios. Code and datasets are available in the following repository: https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
document.input:
Traffic forecasting serves as a cornerstone of multivariate time series analysis and remains a focal point of research within the AI community. To effectively capture the complex spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity inherent in traffic streams, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning, a novel graph structure learning framework designed specifically for spatio-temporal data. We instantiate this approach through the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN), which integrates a Meta-Graph Learner—supported by a specialized Meta-Node Bank—directly into a GCRN encoder-decoder architecture. 

Our framework underwent rigorous evaluation across two standard benchmarks, METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, as well as a newly introduced large-scale dataset, EXPY-TKY, which encompasses 1,843 expressway links in Tokyo. Experimental results demonstrate that MegaCRN consistently outperforms current state-of-the-art models across all three datasets. Furthermore, qualitative analysis reveals that our model successfully disentangles distinct patterns among road links and time intervals, exhibiting robust adaptability to anomalous traffic conditions. The code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
